_comment: Element catalog shared by the web editor (tools/map-editor) and the codegen (tools/gen_maps.py). Adding a new placeable element here teaches BOTH the editor and the build about it. The engine's own rendering of a new decal kind / wallpaper style is still hand-coded in raycast.c; this file is the editor+format+codegen boundary.

cells._doc: grid glyph -> engine world_map cell value

facing._doc: spawn cardinal -> engine angle (0..255). dir=(cos,sin) of angle*360/256; +Y is south. So 0=E(+X) 64=S(+Y) 128=W(-X) 192=N(-Y). Verified vs fixed_map spawn (192, opens north).

partition._doc: free-standing wallpaper divider: a line segment with decor enums

crawl._doc: low-ceiling run -> ceil_h_add_run(cx,cy,dx,dy,len). dir gives dx,dy.

decals._doc: wall-mounted fixed elements. face -> axis (N/S walls run along X => axis 1; E/W walls run along Y => axis 0). z is plate-centre height [0..1]. ADD A NEW WALL ELEMENT BY ADDING AN ENTRY HERE (give it the next kind number).

light._doc: ceiling light fixture placed per-cell in the editor's Lights layer (the bright fluorescent panel + its 3x3 cell_light pool). The engine auto-derives lights today, so authored lights are editor/preview-only until per-map light support lands in the ROM.

limits._doc: must match the engine caps; the codegen rejects maps that exceed these
